title: America’s Missing Fertility Window | Institute for Family Studies
gist: Wendy R. Wang, writing in Compact Magazine, reports the U.S. fertility rate hit a new record low in 2025 per CDC data, but highlights an under-covered shift: women in their 30s now have significantly higher fertility rates than women in their 20s, making the 30s the new peak childbearing decade — a reframing she contrasts with the New York Times' more sanguine 'they'll have babies by 45' narrative.

themes.theme: A record low with a hidden shift

insights.claim: The U.S. fertility rate hit another record low in 2025, according to the most recent CDC data.
insights.speaker: Wendy R. Wang
insights.so_what: Confirms the multi-year fertility downtrend continues unabated as of the latest official data, relevant to any demographic-linked long-horizon consumer or labor-supply model.
insights.quote: The US fertility rate hit yet another new record low in 2025, according to the most recent CDC data.

insights.claim: Women in their 30s now have significantly higher fertility rates than women in their 20s, making the 30s the new peak childbearing decade in the U.S.
insights.speaker: Wendy R. Wang
insights.so_what: A structural shift in the childbearing-age curve with knock-on effects for maternal healthcare demand, fertility-treatment markets, and family-formation timing assumptions in demographic models.
insights.quote: women in their 30s have significantly higher fertility rates than women in their twenties, marking the thirties as the new peak childbearing decade
insights.locator: What is notable is that women

insights.claim: Wang pushes back on the New York Times' sanguine framing that women in their 20s who aren't having babies 'most probably will' by 45, and on media narratives crediting falling teen birth rates as the main culprit for record-low fertility.
insights.speaker: Wendy R. Wang
insights.so_what: Signals a demographer's disagreement with the 'it will catch up later' media narrative — relevant for anyone forecasting completed cohort fertility rather than reacting to annual rate headlines.
insights.quote: Women in their 20s may not be having babies, but by 45 most probably will
insights.locator: The mainstream media’s reaction has been sanguine
